title: 4 Government Bonds
body_text:
4	Government Bonds
4.1	Verifying the Registered Owner芒聙聶s Identity
It is not necessary for the Registered Owner of a bond to have an account at the credit union to redeem a bond. Credit unions are responsible for ensuring that the person signing to redeem a bond is the Registered Owner or a legal representative of the Registered Owner. Observe the following precautions to verify the identity of a Registered Owner.
Individuals
When an individual presents a bond for redemption, obtain at least two pieces of original identification. One of these should have a picture and one should have the Registered Owner芒聙聶s home address. Acceptable identification includes government issued identification (e.g., the individual芒聙聶s driver芒聙聶s licence or passport) and a signed major credit card. Record the individual芒聙聶s address and social insurance number on the bond.
Corporations, Societies, and Other Eligible Entities
If bonds are registered in the name of a corporation, society, or lodge, etc., proceed as follows:
1.
Obtain the signature of the Registered Owner or the legal representative authorized to act on behalf of the corporation, society, etc.
2.
Verify that bonds presented are registered to a corporate member. Redeem bonds in company names for members only.
3.
Verify the bond holder芒聙聶s signature with the company signing officer documents in the corporate client file.
The credit union will be held liable for erroneous payments made due to the credit union芒聙聶s error or negligence.
4.2	Redeeming Government Issued Bonds Registered in the Name of a Minor
If the minor can sign the government bond, handle the bond as if the owner were of the age of majority (see Chapter 5, Redeeming Certificate-style Canada Savings Bonds).
Note:	The age of majority is not the same in all provinces. In BC, the age of majority is 19. In Ontario, the age of majority is 18.
If the minor is unable to sign the government bond, obtain the signature of a parent, guardian, or tutor with legal control and custody of the minor along with one of the following:
A Birth Certificate (verify the minor芒聙聶s age)
A Certificate of Baptism (if the minor was born and baptized in the province of Quebec)
A Notarial Copy of guardianship or tutorship papers
4.3	Redeeming Canada Savings Bonds without the Registered Owner芒聙聶s Signature
This information applies only to Canada Savings Bonds. All other bonds require the signature of the Registered Owner or legal representative. Canada Savings Bonds may only be redeemed without the Registered Owner芒聙聶s signature if
the credit union has signed an agreement with Central 1 to previously sell Canada Savings Bonds (see Section 3.1, Policy for Redeeming or Transferring Bonds through Central 1), and
the bond is stamped by the credit union stating that the proceeds are credited to the Registered Owner.
If a bond is not signed by the Registered Owner, proceed as follows:
1.
Obtain written instructions from the Registered Owner or legal representative giving specific authority to credit bond proceeds
to an account solely in the Registered Owner芒聙聶s name,
to an account in the names of all joint Registered Owners,
to the estate account of a deceased Registered Owner, or
by cheque to the Registered Owner(s) or his/her/their legal representative(s).
2.
Record the manner of settlement on the back of the bond and retain a copy on file.
4.4	Guarantee and Over-Guarantee of Signatures
When a member presents a Government of Canada Marketable Bond to be redeemed, transferred, or sold, the credit union must first guarantee the bond holder芒聙聶s signature. The credit union then forwards the bond to Central 1 as per credit union policy by courier.
Central 1 Over-Guarantee
An over-guarantee is required to transfer bonds, to process the Letter of Name Guarantee, and to redeem Government of Canada marketable bonds.
To obtain Central 1芒聙聶s guarantee, the credit union must provide Central聽1 with a list of designated signing authorities and ensure that the list is updated on a regular basis. The list must appear on credit union letterhead and provide the following information:
name (in full) of each signing authority,
position(s) held in the credit union, and
signature(s) of the signing authorities.
To be valid, the list must be signed under seal by two authorized signing authorities currently on file with Central 1.
For bonds registered to a company or charitable organization when a notarized copy of their Resolution and signing officers isn芒聙聶t available, Central 1芒聙聶s over-guarantee can still be obtained. Central 1 requires the following:
Signatures of the person(s) authorized to sign for the company or charitable organization
The credit union to type or write above or below the signature of registered owners:
芒聢聮
Signature of Transferor and Authority to Sign Guaranteed
芒聢聮
Deposit to the Credit of the Payee Only
The date in the data area on the back of the bond
The credit union芒聙聶s branch stamp on the back of the bond
The credit union to over-guarantee the bondholder芒聙聶s signatures via a signing officer currently on file with Central 1
Central 1 will then over-guarantee one signature, stamp the bond accordingly, and signature guarantee the last remaining signature.
If any of the required items are missing, the bonds will be returned. The Government of Canada will request for proof that the bonds have been signed by the authorized signers of the company or charitable organization.
4.5	Adding Co-Owner Names and Survivor to Co-Owner on Government of Canada Issued Bonds
Registered Owners can add a co-owner芒聙聶s name 芒聙聹and Survivor芒聙聺 or add 芒聙聹and Survivor芒聙聺 to a co-owner芒聙聶s name on Canada Savings Bonds, Canada Premium Bonds, non-certificated bonds, or a Payroll Savings Plan.
The table below shows examples of requests.
Request
Action
Registered Owner (e.g., John Doe) requests the addition of his co-owner芒聙聶s name (e.g., Charlie Brown) with rights of survivorship to his Bond (Government of Canada Retail Debt product)
John Doe completes Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF 533)
John Doe indicates John Doe and Charlie Brown and Survivor in the Transfer section
John Doe signs Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF 533)
John Doe has his signature properly guaranteed (Final Declaration at the bottom of Transfer of Ownership Form TRF 533)
Registered Owners (e.g., John Doe and Charlie Brown) request the addition of right of survivorship to their Bond (Government of Canada Retail Debt product)
John Doe and Charlie Brown complete Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF 533)
John Doe and Charlie Brown indicate John Doe and Charlie Brown and Survivor in the Transfer section
John Doe and Charlie Brown sign Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF533)
John Doe and Charlie Brown have their signatures properly guaranteed (Final Declaration at the bottom of Transfer of Ownership Form TRF 533)
The Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF 533) is available from the Bank of Canada Unclaimed Properties Office website. See the Bank of Canada Unclaimed Properties Office website at
https://www.unclaimedproperties.bankofcanada.ca/
. Figure 1 provides general information about the form and any legal documentation requirements for the transfer of ownership. Figure 2 shows the first page of the 4-page form which consist of Sections A to D.
Figure 1: Guidelines for Completing the Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF 533), Page 1 of 4
Figure 2: Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF 533), Page 2 of 4 for Sections A to D
Proceed as follows to make changes:
1.
Ensure that the Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF 533) is completed by the Registered Owner(s) or the account owner(s) requesting the change.
2.
Provide the following information:
The bond serial numbers for Canada Savings Bonds
The bond serial numbers for Canada Premium Bonds
The portfolio identification for non-certificated security (NCS)
The account number for Canada Savings Bonds
3.
Ensure that the member is aware of the following stipulations
:
Co-owners and the words 芒聙聹and Survivor芒聙聺 can be added to the registration of the Bonds (retail debit products). The individual being added must be a bona fide resident of Canada (more than one co-owner can be added).
Once a name is added to a bond (retail debit product) and the transaction is processed, the name cannot be removed.
By adding 芒聙聹and Survivor芒聙聺 to the product, the co-owner芒聙聶s portion of the product will go to the other co-owner(s) in the event of death (this is not applicable to residents of Quebec).
Where a deceased co-owner is a resident of Quebec, the words 芒聙聹and Survivor芒聙聺 will not be legally recognized. The proceeds from the bonds registered to the deceased Quebec resident will be distributed according to the deceased芒聙聶s Will.
4.
Forward the Bond (Government of Canada Retail Debit product), Transfer of Ownership Form (TRF 533), and correspondence to Central 1 for an over-guarantee as per internal credit union transportation policy.
4.6	Handling Non-Certified Instruments (NCI)
Changes to non-certified instruments (NCI) must be made through the Bank of Canada.
Canada Savings Bonds (CSB) have not been available for purchase through a Payroll Savings Plan since November 1, 2017. However, the funds in your Payroll Savings Plan are safe, guaranteed, and will continue to earn interest until they reach maturity or are redeemed, whichever comes first. Once all the funds have been redeemed or paid out at maturity, your account will be closed. For more information about non-certificated bonds, refer to the Canada Savings Bonds Program at
http://www.csb.gc.ca
.
4.7	Handling Suspect, Fraudulent, or Mutilated Bonds or Coupons
If the credit union has any reason to believe that a bond or Coupon presented for redemption is presumed to be stolen, or if it appears altered, mutilated or defaced, forward the item on collection, along with a letter explaining the circumstances, to the Bank of Canada at the following address:
Bank of Canada
Wholesale Debt Unit, 6
th
Floor
234 Laurier Ave West
Ottawa ON K1A 0G9
If the issuer refuses settlement or provides only partial settlement, Central 1 will provide the credit union with a copy of the issuer芒聙聶s explanation.
4.8	Preparing Tax Statements
The Bank of Canada Unclaimed Properties Office芒聙聶s Online Tool and Redemption Value Tables (S40) specify which tax forms are required when redeeming each series of Canada Savings Bonds. If a tax form is not specified (i.e., Series 45 or later), the Bank of Canada will issue a T5 information slip directly to the Registered Owner. See Chapter 7, Redeeming Marketable Bonds (Government of Canada Bonds and Treasury Bills) at Maturity.
Proceed as follows, after completing the applicable tax form.
T600 Ownership Certificate
1.
Stamp the completed form with the credit union芒聙聶s Endorsement Stamp.
2.
Send a copy to the district taxation office in the credit union芒聙聶s area by the 15th of the month following negotiation/credit (see the CRA website for a list of regional offices).
3.
Give two copies to the Registered Owner.
4.
Retain a copy in the credit union file.
For information on completing forms NR601 and NR602, see Guide T4061, NR4 - Non-Resident Tax Withholding, Remitting, and Reporting. Guide T4061 is available on the CRA website.
NR601 Non-Resident Ownership Certificate 芒聙聯 Withholding Tax
This form is used when a member designated as a non-resident redeems any types of bonds other than Government of Canada or provincial bonds (e.g., corporate bonds or stocks). Non-resident tax at the applicable rate for the member country of residence is deductible on interest earned on these types of bonds.
1.
Stamp the completed form with the credit union芒聙聶s Endorsement Stamp.
2.
Send a copy (by the 15th of the month following negotiation/credit) to
International and Ottawa Tax Services Office
Post Office Box 0769, Station T
Ottawa ON K1G 3Y4
3.
Give a copy to the Registered Owner.
4.
Retain a copy for annual reconciliation and reporting with non-resident account tax.
All interest paid and tax withheld must be remitted monthly and/or reported annually to CRA.
NR602 Non-Resident Ownership Certificate 芒聙聯 No Withholding Tax
This form is used for Government of Canada or provincial bonds being cashed in by a member designated as a non-resident. Non-resident tax is not deductible on interest earned on government instructions. However, amounts paid to a non-resident must be reported to CRA annually.
1.
Send a copy (by the 15th of the month following negotiation/credit) to
International and Ottawa Tax Services Office
Post Office Box 0769, Station T
Ottawa ON K1G 3Y4
2.
Give a copy to the Registered Owner.
3.
Retain a copy for annual reconciliation and reporting with non-resident account tax.
All interest paid to a non-resident must be reported annually to CRA.
4.9	Change of Address for Canada Savings Bonds
To ensure that owners of all series of Regular Interest 芒聙聹R芒聙聺 Canada Savings Bonds and Series 45 and subsequent issues of Compound Interest 芒聙聹C芒聙聺 Canada Savings Bonds receive an annual T5 interest statement and, if applicable, an interest cheque, the owners should advise the Bank of Canada immediately of any address change.
The Change of Address Form (COA), including guidelines, is available from the Bank of Canada Unclaimed Properties Office website. See the Resources for Bond Owners webpage at
https://www.unclaimedproperties.bankofcanada.ca/app/resources-bond-owners
. The first page of the document provides guidelines for completing the form. See Figure 4 for page 1 of the guidelines. The following pages of the document include Sections A to D of the form. See Figure 5 for page 1 of the form.
To make a change of address, complete Sections A, B, C, and D and mail or courier the document as directed on the form.
Note:	Requests containing legal documents cannot be faxed, since original documents with original signatures are required.
Figure 3: Guidelines for Completing the Change of Address (COA), Page 1
Figure 5: Change of Address  Form (COA), Page 1 of 3 for Sections A to D
END
